title: heterotheca villosa var. horrida
intro: Heterotheca villosa var. horrida, also known as rough greenthread, is a perennial plant native to the Great Plains region of North America, characterized by its hairy stems and yellow or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Heterotheca villosa var. horrida include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
